hero.title: Zhijun Zuo Research Group
hero.subtitle: Photoredox, radical, and asymmetric metal catalysis in synthesis.111

groups.note:
Zhijun was born in Luzhou, China. He received his B.S. degree from Northwest University, Xi’an, China, in 2013. He then pursued his Ph.D. studies at the same institution under the supervision of Prof. Xinjun Luan, focusing on transition-metal-catalyzed dearomatization cyclization reactions.
After obtaining his Ph.D. degree in 2018, he moved to the west coast of the United States to conduct postdoctoral research on asymmetric catalysis under the guidance of Prof. Barry M. Trost at Stanford University.
In 2021, he was awarded the Alexander von Humboldt Fellowship and joined the group of Prof. Armido Studer at the University of Münster, where he expanded his research interests into radical chemistry.
In 2022, he decided to pursue an independent academic career and returned to his alma mater.
Future format: name, degree, graduation year, and current position.
